meta.intro: Google quietly scrapped Gemini 3.5 Pro's base model and rebuilt it from scratch — costing Alphabet $200B in a day — while Anthropic is in talks to rent $10B of compute from Meta, a direct rival, and Nvidia's flagship rack just slipped a full year with no fix in sight.
meta.briefingTitle: The Roadmaps Are Slipping. The Money Isn't Waiting.
meta.summary: Three of today's biggest global stories are the same story: engineering timelines are breaking under the weight of the AI trade's expectations. Google scrapped and rebuilt Gemini 3.5 Pro from scratch, costing Alphabet $200B in a day; Nvidia's flagship Kyber rack slipped a full year with 'no proven solution' yet; and Anthropic is now in talks to lease $10B of compute from Meta, a direct model rival, because no single vendor can keep up. Money keeps moving anyway — a pre-product DeepMind spinout just priced at $300M, and xAI turned a data-exfiltration scandal into an open-source release within days. Japan's throughline is the same gap in miniature: a revised national AI plan formally cedes the frontier race to focus on robotics and vertical AI, Fujitsu just built a four-company coalition to own that space — entirely on Nvidia's own stack — and Kioxia lost half its market cap to a US courtroom loss stacked on chip-market jitters.

gaps.label: Frontier models
gaps.note: Japan's revised AI Basic Plan (Jul 14) explicitly cedes the frontier-model race to focus on vertical and physical AI instead — a strategic retreat, not a capability gain, in the same week Google scrapped and retrained Gemini 3.5 Pro from scratch.

gaps.label: Semiconductors (logic vs. memory)
gaps.note: Kioxia lost half its market cap in a month — a Texas jury's $229M Viasat patent verdict stacked on a chip-stock rout — while TSMC added another $100B to Arizona, including its first advanced-packaging fab built outside Taiwan.

gaps.label: AI venture funding
gaps.note: A DeepMind alum raised $55M at a $300M pre-product valuation in San Francisco; Japan's comparable-stage raises (InnoJin's ¥230M healthcare-AI Series C) run one to two orders of magnitude smaller and are still routed through corporate strategics, not pure VC.

gaps.label: Robotics & physical AI
gaps.note: Fujitsu, Fanuc, Yaskawa and Kawasaki just formed Japan's first real cross-brand robot-orchestration coalition — but it runs entirely on Nvidia's Cosmos/Omniverse/Isaac stack, the same dependency as every Western robotics player it's meant to compete with.

items.headline: Google Scrapped Gemini 3.5 Pro's Base Model From Scratch — Alphabet Lost $200B in a Day
items.dek: A Bloomberg report that Google rebuilt its flagship model after finding coding and reasoning failures wiped out roughly $200B in Alphabet market cap in one session.

analysis.happening: Alphabet fell 4.4% (~$200B) on Jul 16 after Bloomberg reported Gemini 3.5 Pro is months behind its May unveiling; engineers reportedly found failures in recursive tool-calling and SVG generation serious enough to scrap the base model and retrain from scratch.
analysis.now: The market priced in something more specific than a slipped date — a real coding/reasoning gap versus OpenAI and Anthropic, not just scheduling. Caveat: the technical detail comes from unnamed sources via Bloomberg, not a Google admission.
analysis.future: Watch for Google's next stated ship date, and whether Alphabet's earnings call addresses AI model cadence directly — a second miss would be a far bigger signal than this one.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.title: Alphabet shares fall on report its most powerful AI model Gemini 3.5 Pro is delayed

sources.name: Bloomberg
sources.title: Google Gemini Launch Delayed as Tech Falls Short of Internal Goals

items.headline: Anthropic Is in Talks to Lease $10B of Compute — From Meta, a Rival
items.dek: Anthropic wants to rent Meta's data centers for two years, the latest leg of a compute-diversification spree spanning Google, Microsoft, SpaceX — and now possibly Meta.

analysis.happening: First reported Jul 17: Anthropic proposed in June, and Meta is now evaluating, a roughly $10B two-year compute lease with early-exit options — alongside Anthropic's existing $40B/5GW Google TPU deal, $30B Microsoft/Nvidia Azure commitment, and $1.25B/month SpaceX GPU deal.
analysis.now: Meta building AI infrastructure and renting spare capacity to a direct model rival signals hyperscaler capex has outrun even Meta's own model roadmap. For Anthropic, five vendors is both a supply hedge and a pricing lever. Caveat: still unsigned, early talks.
analysis.future: If it closes, the leaked price-per-GPU-hour terms are the real data point — the actual read on compute economics across Nvidia, TPU and Meta's own silicon.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.title: Anthropic in early talks with Meta to acquire compute power

sources.name: TheNextWeb
sources.title: Anthropic is in early talks to lease $10 billion in compute from Meta

items.headline: Nvidia's Top Rack Slips to 2028 — and It Has No Fix Yet
items.dek: The Kyber NVL144 rack for Rubin Ultra is delayed over a year on a bad circuit board, and Nvidia killed its stopgap too — a real opening for AMD and Google.

analysis.happening: SemiAnalysis reports Nvidia's Kyber rack — 144 Rubin Ultra GPUs per cabinet — slipped from 2027 to 2028 over a hard-to-manufacture 78-layer PCB midplane. Nvidia also cancelled its NVL72x2 stopgap after customer pushback; CNBC and Tom's Hardware confirmed the reporting.
analysis.now: First time since Blackwell that Nvidia has had no working answer at the top of its rack roadmap for 12+ months — a dated opening for AMD's MI500X and Google's TPU systems to lock in commitments Nvidia can't fill. Caveat: only the top-tier rack is affected.
analysis.future: Watch for any AMD or Google TPU order announcements from Microsoft, Meta or xAI during the gap, and whether Nvidia shows a fix before its next GTC.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.title: Nvidia's Kyber rack system delayed to 2028 over manufacturing snags

sources.name: Tom's Hardware
sources.title: Nvidia's Kyber rack for Rubin Ultra reportedly delayed to 2028, stopgap solution also axed due to customer pushback

items.headline: xAI Open-Sources Its Coding Agent — Days After It Was Caught Exfiltrating Repos
items.dek: xAI released the full Rust codebase of its terminal coding agent under Apache 2.0, days after a researcher showed it was silently uploading users' repos — API keys included — to an xAI-controlled cloud bucket.

analysis.happening: A researcher published a wire-level analysis showing Grok Build, xAI's CLI coding agent, packaged users' full git repos — commit history, API keys, DB passwords — into bundles shipped to a Google Cloud bucket xAI controlled, ~27,800x the data volume the task needed. Four days later xAI open-sourced the code and reset data retention to off.
analysis.now: Reads as crisis PR, not generosity — researchers reading the published source found the exfiltration code still there, just gated behind a server-side flag xAI controls. Worth reading any CLI agent's networking code before trusting its defaults.
analysis.future: Watch whether xAI removes the exfiltration path entirely rather than just flagging it off, and whether this prompts audits of other CLI coding agents for the same silent-upload pattern.

sources.name: The Register
sources.title: SpaceX open sources Grok Build in same week company was found beaming users' repos to the cloud

sources.name: xAI
sources.title: Grok Build is Now Open Source

items.headline: A DeepMind Alum Raised $55M at a $300M Valuation Before Writing a Line of Product Code
items.dek: Andrew Dai left 14 years at Google DeepMind to chase 'visual AGI,' and Menlo Ventures, Nvidia and Jeff Dean bet on pedigree alone.

analysis.happening: Elorian, founded by 14-year DeepMind veteran Andrew Dai and ex-Apple researcher Yinfei Yang, raised a $55M seed at a $300M post-money valuation, co-led by Striker Ventures, Menlo Ventures and Altimeter Capital, with Nvidia as strategic investor and Google's Jeff Dean as an angel.
analysis.now: It validates a thesis that visual/spatial reasoning is the next scaling frontier — and that founder pedigree alone can command a nine-figure pre-product valuation. Caveat: nothing has shipped; this exact pattern has fizzled before for other 'genius premoney' rounds.
analysis.future: Watch for Elorian's first commercial pilots (manufacturing/automation is the stated first vertical), and whether other frontier-lab alumni try to replicate the pre-product premium.

sources.name: TechCrunch
sources.title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product

sources.name: MLQ
sources.title: Elorian Raises $55M Seed at $300M Valuation for Visual AI Before Shipping a Product

items.headline: Fujitsu, Fanuc, Yaskawa and Kawasaki Form a Physical-AI Coalition — on Nvidia's Rails
items.dek: Japan's four biggest robotics and IT names agreed to jointly build a 'sovereign' robot-control layer on Nvidia's Cosmos/Omniverse/Isaac stack, betting on owning orchestration rather than humanoid hardware.

analysis.happening: Fujitsu announced joint studies with Fanuc, Yaskawa Electric and Kawasaki Heavy Industries (Jul 16) to build a shared control/task-planning platform linking business software to robot control across brands, running on Nvidia's Cosmos, Omniverse/Isaac and Newton — Fujitsu says the control layer stays 'sovereign' and will open to allied firms.
analysis.now: US players race on humanoid hardware and end-to-end model control; Japan supplies ~45% of the world's industrial robots but lacked cross-brand AI orchestration — this bets the control layer, not the body, is Japan's defensible moat, even though the silicon underneath is entirely Nvidia's. Caveat: still a 'business study,' no pilot or date set.
analysis.future: Watch for the first named pilot — a Fanuc line, Yaskawa warehouse, Kawasaki hospital — and whether the platform actually opens beyond its four founders.

items.headline: Kioxia Loses Half Its Market Cap in a Month — a US Jury Verdict, Not Just China's Kimi K3
items.dek: A $229M Viasat patent verdict plus a Philadelphia semiconductor rout wiped roughly ¥30 trillion off Kioxia in a single day, knocking it from Japan's most valuable stock to fifth.

analysis.happening: Kioxia hit limit-down Jul 17, falling to ¥52,110 — less than half its June high of ¥112,700, market cap down from near ¥60T to under ¥30T. A Texas jury found Kioxia infringed Viasat flash patents ($229M/¥37B damages) the same day the SOX fell over 4%, dragging the Nikkei down 2,694 points.
analysis.now: Japan's memory sector is now trading on two decoupled risks — US courtroom exposure layered on macro AI-capex jitters — not just the China open-weight shock already covered this week. Caveat: analysts framed it as a leverage/margin unwind, not a collapse of the underlying AI-demand thesis.
analysis.future: Watch Kioxia's appeal or settlement of the Viasat verdict, and whether other US flash-patent holders file copycat suits given Kioxia's cash-rich profile.

items.headline: Japan Rewrites Its Six-Month-Old AI Plan to Cut Reliance on 'Specific Countries or Companies'
items.dek: An emergency Cabinet revision — adopted the same week China launched a rival AI governance body Japan didn't join — orders Japan's AI Safety Institute expanded and prioritizes vertical and physical AI over racing the frontier.

analysis.happening: Japan's Cabinet revised its six-month-old AI Basic Plan on Jul 14: avoid 'excessive reliance on specific countries or companies,' expand the AI Safety Institute's frontier-model evaluation capacity, and prioritize vertical/physical AI over general frontier models. Two days later, 29 countries — not Japan, the US, EU or Korea — launched China's rival WAICO governance body.
analysis.now: Read between the lines: Japan is naming, without naming, its dependence on OpenAI, Anthropic, Google and Nvidia — the same week it committed to a homegrown model and formed the Fujitsu robotics coalition. Caveat: guidelines aren't law — no fines or bans yet under Japan's soft-law AI regime.
analysis.future: Watch the new legal-framework council (announced Jul 7) to see if guidelines become hard rules, and whether the Safety Institute's budget actually grows to match its expanded mandate.

sources.name: Japan Times
sources.title: Japan revises AI policy guidelines to bolster cybersecurity

items.headline: Japan's From-Scratch LLM Quietly Reaches 800 City Governments While Nvidia-Weight Rivals Grab Headlines
items.dek: Preferred Networks' PLaMo 3.0 Prime, trained from scratch instead of fine-tuned on Nvidia or Meta weights, is now the default model inside a public-sector AI platform used by roughly 800 Japanese municipalities.

analysis.happening: PFN's PLaMo 3.0 Prime went GA Jun 22 — built with NICT, 256K context, reasoning/non-reasoning variants, available via API, Bedrock, on-prem and Snowflake. It's the default model inside 'QommonsAI,' now adopted by roughly 800 municipalities, and one of the domestic LLMs in the Digital Agency's GENNAI environment.
analysis.now: Most of Japan's visible AI names fine-tune Nvidia or Meta open weights; PFN still trains from scratch and has real, if unglamorous, GovTech distribution instead of consumer chat. Caveat: PLaMo trails GPT-5-class, Claude and Gemini by a real margin, and 800 municipalities measures wrapper reach, not necessarily heavy usage.
analysis.future: Watch PFN's next PLaMo version, and whether it pushes beyond GovTech into private enterprise, where Nvidia-weight-based domestic rivals are moving faster.
